1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## What This Directory Is
     6→
     7→RISC-V architecture-specific math library (`libm`) optimizations for picolibc. These replace generic C implementations from `libm/common/` and `libm/math/` with hand-tuned assembly or hardware-FPU-aware C code.
     8→
     9→See `README.md` in this directory for detailed function listings and build configuration matrix.
    10→
    11→## Build System
    12→
    13→This directory is built as part of the top-level picolibc Meson build. There is no standalone build. The `meson.build` here populates `srcs_libm_machine` which gets compiled into the libm static library.
    14→
    15→A separate `CMakeLists.txt` exists for CMake-based builds but only includes a minimal subset (C files, no assembly, no configuration branching).
    16→
    17→### Key build flags (set in top-level `meson.build`, consumed here)
    18→
    19→- `is_softfp` — software floating-point ABI (no hardware FPU)
    20→- `is_double_abi` — hardware double-precision FPU
    21→- `is_riscv64` — 64-bit RISC-V
    22→- `is_rvm` — M-extension (hardware multiply/divide)
    23→- `is_rv32e` — RV32E reduced 16-register ISA
    24→
    25→### Per-target library quirk
    26→
    27→For soft-float RV32 configs (both standard and RV32E), `sf_sincos.c` is compiled as a separate per-target `static_library` via the `foreach params : targets` loop, not added to `srcs_libm_machine`. This is because it needs per-multilib `c_args`. The resulting `lib_machine<target>` libraries are linked via `link_whole` elsewhere.
    28→
    29→### Internal call graph
    30→
    31→Assembly math functions call shared Andes helper routines (`andes_*.s`). Key dependency chains:
    32→- Trig functions → `__gf_dpreduct`/`__gf_fpreduct` (argument reduction) → `__gf_exmul`/`__gf_exadd` (extended precision) → polynomial evaluators (`__gf_p4`–`__gf_p8`)
    33→- `exp`/`log`/`pow` → `__gf_dpexexp`/`__gf_dpexlog` → extended precision helpers
    34→- All assembly shares coefficient tables: `__gf_dpsincon`, `__gf_dpcoscon`, `__gf_fpsincof`, `__gf_fpcoscof`, etc.
    35→
    36→
    37→## Standalone Test Builds (outside Meson)
    38→
    39→For quick accuracy/correctness testing of individual assembly routines, use the standalone `Makefile` in this directory. It compiles test C files and links them against the relevant assembly objects directly.
    40→
    41→### Toolchain
    42→
    43→Use the Andes `nds32le-elf-mculib-v5` toolchain for RV32 soft-float builds:
    44→
    45→```
    46→/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5/bin/riscv32-elf-gcc
    47→```
    48→
    49→- GCC 14.2.0, bare-metal (`riscv32-elf`)
    50→- Ships with newlib (provides printf, math reference functions, etc.)
    51→- `-mvh` flag enables Andes virtual hosting (semihosting) for I/O via QEMU/GDB
    52→- Target flags: `-march=rv32im -mabi=ilp32` for soft-float M-extension builds
    53→
    54→### QEMU
    55→
    56→Use the Andes QEMU v5 build for running RV32 test ELFs:
    57→
    58→```
    59→/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
    60→```
    61→
    62→- QEMU 9.2.0 (Andes fork, v9.2.0-401-g4581fc99a4)
    63→- Uses the `qemu-runner` agent type in Claude Code to handle semihosting output capture
    64→
    65→### Example: build and run sinf test
    66→
    67→```sh
    68→make clean && make          # builds both opt and ref ELFs
    69→make compare                # builds, runs both in QEMU, diffs outputs
    70→```
    71→
    72→The Makefile builds two ELFs: `test_sinf_opt.elf` (linked with Andes assembly) and `test_sinf_ref.elf` (linked with toolchain's libm only). The `compare` target runs both through QEMU and diffs the output.
    73→
    74→### Unconditionally compiled sources
    75→
    76→Regardless of build configuration, the following C files are always included: `s_copysign`, `s_finite`, `s_fma`, `s_isinf`, `s_isnan`, `s_llrint`, `s_llround`, and their `sf_` (float) counterparts plus `sf_fmax`, `sf_fmin`, `sf_lrint`, `sf_lround`.
    77→
    78→## Naming Conventions
    79→
    80→- `e_` — IEEE error-handling wrapper functions (e.g., `e_sqrt` wraps the core sqrt)
    81→- `s_` — double-precision standard functions
    82→- `sf_` — single-precision (float) standard functions
    83→- `k_` — kernel helpers (internal, e.g., `k_sin`, `k_cos`)
    84→- `er_` — reentrant variants (e.g., `er_lgamma`)
    85→- `andes_` — shared assembly building blocks (not standalone math functions)
    86→

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What This Directory Is

RISC-V architecture-specific math library (`libm`) optimizations for picolibc. These replace generic C implementations from `libm/common/` and `libm/math/` with hand-tuned assembly or hardware-FPU-aware C code.

See `README.md` in this directory for detailed function listings and build configuration matrix.

## Build System

This directory is built as part of the top-level picolibc Meson build. There is no standalone build. The `meson.build` here populates `srcs_libm_machine` which gets compiled into the libm static library.

A separate `CMakeLists.txt` exists for CMake-based builds but only includes a minimal subset (C files, no assembly, no configuration branching).

### Key build flags (set in top-level `meson.build`, consumed here)

- `is_softfp` — software floating-point ABI (no hardware FPU)
- `is_double_abi` — hardware double-precision FPU
- `is_riscv64` — 64-bit RISC-V
- `is_rvm` — M-extension (hardware multiply/divide)
- `is_rv32e` — RV32E reduced 16-register ISA

### Per-target library quirk

For soft-float RV32 configs (both standard and RV32E), `sf_sincos.c` is compiled as a separate per-target `static_library` via the `foreach params : targets` loop, not added to `srcs_libm_machine`. This is because it needs per-multilib `c_args`. The resulting `lib_machine<target>` libraries are linked via `link_whole` elsewhere.

### Internal call graph

Assembly math functions call shared Andes helper routines (`andes_*.s`). Key dependency chains:
- Trig functions → `__gf_dpreduct`/`__gf_fpreduct` (argument reduction) → `__gf_exmul`/`__gf_exadd` (extended precision) → polynomial evaluators (`__gf_p4`–`__gf_p8`)
- `exp`/`log`/`pow` → `__gf_dpexexp`/`__gf_dpexlog` → extended precision helpers
- All assembly shares coefficient tables: `__gf_dpsincon`, `__gf_dpcoscon`, `__gf_fpsincof`, `__gf_fpcoscof`, etc.


## Standalone Test Builds (outside Meson)

For quick accuracy/correctness testing of individual assembly routines, use the standalone `Makefile` in this directory. It compiles test C files and links them against the relevant assembly objects directly.

### Toolchain

Use the Andes `nds32le-elf-mculib-v5` toolchain for RV32 soft-float builds:

```
/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5/bin/riscv32-elf-gcc
```

- GCC 14.2.0, bare-metal (`riscv32-elf`)
- Ships with newlib (provides printf, math reference functions, etc.)
- `-mvh` flag enables Andes virtual hosting (semihosting) for I/O via QEMU/GDB
- Target flags: `-march=rv32im -mabi=ilp32` for soft-float M-extension builds

### QEMU

Use the Andes QEMU v5 build for running RV32 test ELFs:

```
/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
```

- QEMU 9.2.0 (Andes fork, v9.2.0-401-g4581fc99a4)
- Uses the `qemu-runner` agent type in Claude Code to handle semihosting output capture

### Example: build and run sinf test

```sh
make clean && make          # builds both opt and ref ELFs
make compare                # builds, runs both in QEMU, diffs outputs
```

The Makefile builds two ELFs: `test_sinf_opt.elf` (linked with Andes assembly) and `test_sinf_ref.elf` (linked with toolchain's libm only). The `compare` target runs both through QEMU and diffs the output.

### Unconditionally compiled sources

Regardless of build configuration, the following C files are always included: `s_copysign`, `s_finite`, `s_fma`, `s_isinf`, `s_isnan`, `s_llrint`, `s_llround`, and their `sf_` (float) counterparts plus `sf_fmax`, `sf_fmin`, `sf_lrint`, `sf_lround`.

## Naming Conventions

- `e_` — IEEE error-handling wrapper functions (e.g., `e_sqrt` wraps the core sqrt)
- `s_` — double-precision standard functions
- `sf_` — single-precision (float) standard functions
- `k_` — kernel helpers (internal, e.g., `k_sin`, `k_cos`)
- `er_` — reentrant variants (e.g., `er_lgamma`)
- `andes_` — shared assembly building blocks (not standalone math functions)

content.content:
1→# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
     2→# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
     3→#
     4→# Usage:
     5→#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
     6→#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
     7→#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
     8→#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
     9→#
    10→# Toolchain selection:
    11→#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
    12→#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain
    13→
    14→XLEN ?= 32
    15→
    16→ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
    17→  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
    18→  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
    19→  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
    20→  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
    21→  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
    22→else
    23→  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
    24→  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
    25→  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
    26→  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
    27→  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
    28→endif
    29→
    30→CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    31→AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    32→LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc
    33→
    34→CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
    35→ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
    36→LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
    37→LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes
    38→
    39→SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
    40→NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
    41→AR   = $(PREFIX)ar
    42→
    43→# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
    44→ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
    45→ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)
    46→
    47→# Assembly sources — single-precision
    48→ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
    49→           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
    50→           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
    51→           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
    52→           sf_fpclassify.s
    53→ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    54→
    55→# Assembly sources — double-precision
    56→S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
    57→             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
    58→             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
    59→             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
    60→             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
    61→             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
    62→S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)
    63→
    64→# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
    65→# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.
    66→
    67→# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
    68→FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
    69→               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
    70→               modff fpclassifyf
    71→
    72→# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
    73→FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
    74→                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
    75→                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo
    76→
    77→# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
    78→FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
    79→                     hypot remainder lrint lround
    80→
    81→# All functions to test
    82→FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)
    83→
    84→# Test output directory
    85→TESTDIR = test
    86→
    87→# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
    88→V ?= 0
    89→ifeq ($(V),1)
    90→  Q =
    91→  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
    92→  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
    93→  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
    94→  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
    95→else
    96→  Q = @
    97→  MSG_AS =
    98→  MSG_CC =
    99→  MSG_LD =
   100→  MSG_AR =
   101→endif
   102→
   103→libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
   104→	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^
   105→
   106→# --- Pattern rules ---
   107→
   108→%.o: %.s
   109→	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
   110→
   111→$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
   112→	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<
   113→
   114→# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
   115→# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
   116→# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
   117→define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
   118→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
   119→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)
   120→
   121→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   122→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   123→
   124→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   125→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   126→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   127→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   128→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   129→	else \
   130→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   131→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   132→	fi
   133→
   134→size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
   135→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
   136→endef
   137→
   138→# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
   139→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
   140→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
   141→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)
   142→
   143→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   144→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   145→
   146→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   147→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   148→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
   149→	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
   150→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   151→	else \
   152→		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
   153→			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
   154→	fi
   155→
   156→size-$(1): s_$(1).o
   157→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
   158→endef
   159→
   160→# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
   161→define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
   162→$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
   163→	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)
   164→
   165→test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
   166→	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
   167→	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
   168→		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
   169→	else \
   170→		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
   171→	fi
   172→endef
   173→
   174→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
   175→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
   176→$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))
   177→
   178→# --- Top-level targets ---
   179→
   180→OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
   181→           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
   182→REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)
   183→
   184→all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)
   185→
   186→test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
   187→	@echo "Done."
   188→
   189→size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
   190→	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
   191→	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
   192→		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
   193→			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
   194→	@echo "  --------------------"
   195→	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
   196→		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')
   197→
   198→list:
   199→	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
   200→	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"
   201→
   202→clean:
   203→	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt
   204→
   205→.PHONY: all size-all test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
   206→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
   207→        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f))
   208→

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM or RV64IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size-all       - report .text size for each assembly function
#   make size-sinf      - report .text size for a single function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function
#
# Toolchain selection:
#   make XLEN=32 ...    - use RV32 toolchain (default)
#   make XLEN=64 ...    - use RV64 toolchain

XLEN ?= 32

ifeq ($(XLEN),64)
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv64imc -mabi=lp64
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-ax25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
else
  TOOLCHAIN  = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
  PREFIX     = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-
  ARCH       = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
  QEMU       = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
  QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios
endif

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

CFLAGS   = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS  = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS     = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm
LDFLAGS_OPT = $(ARCH) -mvh -L. -landes

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm
AR   = $(PREFIX)ar

# Andes helper assembly (shared internals — not standalone math functions)
ANDES_SRCS = $(wildcard andes_*.s)
ANDES_OBJS = $(ANDES_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_AR  = @printf "  AR\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
  MSG_AR =
endif

libandes.a: $(ANDES_OBJS)
	$(MSG_AR)$(Q)$(AR) rcs $@ $^

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o libandes.a
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$(filter-out libandes.a,$$^) $$(LDFLAGS_OPT)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi

size-$(1): s_$(1).o
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(1) `$$(SIZE) -A $$< | awk '/\.text/ {print $$$$2}'`
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only (no opt to compare)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	$$(Q)$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf < /dev/null > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@if [ -s $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt ]; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL\n" $(1); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size-all: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size-all test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(ANDES_OBJS) libandes.a $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size-all test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),size-$(f)) \
        $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),size-$(f))

### QEMU

Use the Andes QEMU v5 build for running RV32 test ELFs:

```
/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
```

- QEMU 9.2.0 (Andes fork, v9.2.0-401-g4581fc99a4)
- Uses the `qemu-runner` agent type in Claude Code to handle semihosting output capture

### Example: build and run sinf test

```sh
make clean && make          # builds both opt and ref ELFs
make compare                # builds, runs both in QEMU, diffs outputs
```

The Makefile builds two ELFs: `test_sinf_opt.elf` (linked with Andes assembly) and `test_sinf_ref.elf` (linked with toolchain's libm only). The `compare` target runs both through QEMU and diffs the output.
input.new_string:
### QEMU

Use the Andes QEMU v5 build for running test ELFs:

- RV32: `/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32`
- RV64: `/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64`

QEMU 9.2.0 (Andes fork, v9.2.0-401-g4581fc99a4). Run via the Makefile targets below -- do not invoke QEMU directly.

### Makefile targets

```sh
make                        # build all opt and ref ELFs (default: XLEN=32)
make XLEN=64                # build for RV64 toolchain
make test-all               # build, run all funcs in QEMU, diff opt vs ref
make test-sinf              # build and test a single function
make size-all               # report .text size for every assembly function
make size-sinf              # report .text size for one function
make clean                  # remove all build artifacts
make list                   # show all available per-function targets
```

For each function with assembly, the Makefile builds `test/<func>_opt.elf` (linked with Andes assembly + `libandes.a`) and `test/<func>_ref.elf` (linked with toolchain libm only), runs both through QEMU, and diffs the outputs. Functions without assembly (e.g. `acosh`, `hypot`) get a ref-only run.

For each function with assembly, the Makefile builds `test/<func>_opt.elf` (linked with Andes assembly + `libandes.a`) and `test/<func>_ref.elf` (linked with toolchain libm only), runs both through QEMU, and diffs the outputs. Functions without assembly (e.g. `acosh`, `hypot`) get a ref-only run.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What This Directory Is

RISC-V architecture-specific math library (`libm`) optimizations for picolibc. These replace generic C implementations from `libm/common/` and `libm/math/` with hand-tuned assembly or hardware-FPU-aware C code.

See `README.md` in this directory for detailed function listings and build configuration matrix.

## Build System

This directory is built as part of the top-level picolibc Meson build. There is no standalone build. The `meson.build` here populates `srcs_libm_machine` which gets compiled into the libm static library.

A separate `CMakeLists.txt` exists for CMake-based builds but only includes a minimal subset (C files, no assembly, no configuration branching).

### Key build flags (set in top-level `meson.build`, consumed here)

- `is_softfp` — software floating-point ABI (no hardware FPU)
- `is_double_abi` — hardware double-precision FPU
- `is_riscv64` — 64-bit RISC-V
- `is_rvm` — M-extension (hardware multiply/divide)
- `is_rv32e` — RV32E reduced 16-register ISA

### Per-target library quirk

For soft-float RV32 configs (both standard and RV32E), `sf_sincos.c` is compiled as a separate per-target `static_library` via the `foreach params : targets` loop, not added to `srcs_libm_machine`. This is because it needs per-multilib `c_args`. The resulting `lib_machine<target>` libraries are linked via `link_whole` elsewhere.

### Internal call graph

Assembly math functions call shared Andes helper routines (`andes_*.s`). Key dependency chains:
- Trig functions → `__gf_dpreduct`/`__gf_fpreduct` (argument reduction) → `__gf_exmul`/`__gf_exadd` (extended precision) → polynomial evaluators (`__gf_p4`–`__gf_p8`)
- `exp`/`log`/`pow` → `__gf_dpexexp`/`__gf_dpexlog` → extended precision helpers
- All assembly shares coefficient tables: `__gf_dpsincon`, `__gf_dpcoscon`, `__gf_fpsincof`, `__gf_fpcoscof`, etc.


## Standalone Test Builds (outside Meson)

For quick accuracy/correctness testing of individual assembly routines, use the standalone `Makefile` in this directory. It compiles test C files and links them against the relevant assembly objects directly.

### Toolchain

Use the Andes `nds32le-elf-mculib-v5` toolchain for RV32 soft-float builds:

```
/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5/bin/riscv32-elf-gcc
```

- GCC 14.2.0, bare-metal (`riscv32-elf`)
- Ships with newlib (provides printf, math reference functions, etc.)
- `-mvh` flag enables Andes virtual hosting (semihosting) for I/O via QEMU/GDB
- Target flags: `-march=rv32im -mabi=ilp32` for soft-float M-extension builds

### QEMU

Use the Andes QEMU v5 build for running RV32 test ELFs:

```
/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
```

- QEMU 9.2.0 (Andes fork, v9.2.0-401-g4581fc99a4)
- Uses the `qemu-runner` agent type in Claude Code to handle semihosting output capture

### Example: build and run sinf test

```sh
make clean && make          # builds both opt and ref ELFs
make compare                # builds, runs both in QEMU, diffs outputs
```

The Makefile builds two ELFs: `test_sinf_opt.elf` (linked with Andes assembly) and `test_sinf_ref.elf` (linked with toolchain's libm only). The `compare` target runs both through QEMU and diffs the output.

### Unconditionally compiled sources

Regardless of build configuration, the following C files are always included: `s_copysign`, `s_finite`, `s_fma`, `s_isinf`, `s_isnan`, `s_llrint`, `s_llround`, and their `sf_` (float) counterparts plus `sf_fmax`, `sf_fmin`, `sf_lrint`, `sf_lround`.

## Naming Conventions

- `e_` — IEEE error-handling wrapper functions (e.g., `e_sqrt` wraps the core sqrt)
- `s_` — double-precision standard functions
- `sf_` — single-precision (float) standard functions
- `k_` — kernel helpers (internal, e.g., `k_sin`, `k_cos`)
- `er_` — reentrant variants (e.g., `er_lgamma`)
- `andes_` — shared assembly building blocks (not standalone math functions)
